name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
وَٱلطُّورِ (١)
```

```english
By the Mount [Sinai], (1)
```

```arabic
وَكِتَـٰبٍ مَّسْطُورٍ (٢)
```

```english
by the Book inscribed (2)
```

```arabic
فِى رَقٍّ مَّنشُورٍ (٣)
```

```english
on an unrolled parchment; (3)
```

```arabic
وَٱلْبَيْتِ ٱلْمَعْمُورِ (٤)
```

```english
by the House greatly frequented; (4)
```

```arabic
وَٱلسَّقْفِ ٱلْمَرْفُوعِ (٥)
```

```english
by the vault raised high, (5)
```

```arabic
وَٱلْبَحْرِ ٱلْمَسْجُورِ (٦)
```

```english
by the surging sea: (6)
```

```arabic
إِنَّ عَذَابَ رَبِّكَ لَوَٰقِعٌ (٧)
```

```english
indeed your Lord’s punishment will surely befall. (7)
```

```arabic
مَّا لَهُۥ مِن دَافِعٍ (٨)
```

```english
There is none who can avert it. (8)
```

```arabic
يَوْمَ تَمُورُ ٱلسَّمَآءُ مَوْرًا (٩)
```

```english
On the day when the sky whirls violently, (9)
```

```arabic
وَتَسِيرُ ٱلْجِبَالُ سَيْرًا (١٠)
```

```english
and the mountains move with an awful motion: (10)
```

## (Commentary)

The purpose of the Surah is to warn the people of denial and obstinacy among the disbelievers (الكفار) of the punishment prepared for them on the Day of Resurrection. It begins by announcing the occurrence of the punishment they were warned of and its realization on the Day of Resurrection through emphasized oaths and solemn swearing, asserting that it shall not leave them on that day until it befalls them, and there is no escape.

Then, it mentions a portion of the description of this punishment and the woe that will encompass them and never leave them. Subsequently, it contrasts this with a glimpse of the bliss of the people of delight on that day—the God-conscious (المتقون) — *those who maintain an inner state of vigilance and protection against Allah’s displeasure* — who were in the world apprehensive among their folk, supplicating to Allah, believing in Him, and professing His oneness (موحدين له).

Next, it proceeds to rebuke the deniers for what they used to charge the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله with, and for their rejection of what was revealed to him of the Quran and the true religion (الدين الحق) he brought.

The discourse concludes by repeating the threat and the promise of punishment, and by commanding the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله to glorify his Lord. The Surah is Meccan, as evidenced by the context of its verses.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَالطُّورِ** **By the Mount,** It has been said that *al-Tur* (الطور) refers to any mountain in general, but its usage has become predominant for the mountain upon which Allah spoke to Moses عليه‌السلام. It is more appropriate that the intended meaning in the verse is the Mount of Moses عليه‌السلام; Allah the Exalted swore by it because He sanctified it and blessed it, just as He swore by it in His saying: **وَطُورِ سِينِينَ** **and Mount Sinai,** (95:2). He also said: **وَنادَيْناهُ مِنْ جانِبِ الطُّورِ الْأَيْمَنِ** **We called him from the right side of the Mount,** (19:52), and He said in His address to Moses عليه‌السلام: **فَاخْلَعْ نَعْلَيْكَ إِنَّكَ بِالْوادِ الْمُقَدَّسِ طُوىً** **so take off your sandals; you are indeed in the holy valley of Tuwa.** (20:12). He further said: **نُودِيَ مِنْ شاطِئِ الْوادِ الْأَيْمَنِ فِي الْبُقْعَةِ الْمُبارَكَةِ مِنَ الشَّجَرَةِ** **he was called from the right bank of the valley in the blessed site from the tree:** (28:30).

It has also been said that the intended meaning is any mountain in general; Allah the Exalted swore by it because of the various types of His blessings He deposited therein. The Exalted said: **وَجَعَلَ فِيها رَواسِيَ مِنْ فَوْقِها وَبارَكَ فِيها** **He set in it firm mountains [rising] above it, and blessed it** (41:10).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَكِتابٍ مَسْطُورٍ فِي رَقٍّ مَنْشُورٍ** **by the Book inscribed on unrolled parchment,** It is said that **parchment (الرق)** is anything written upon, and it is said to be paper, or paper made from skin. **Unrolling (النشر)** is the act of spreading out and dispersing.

Regarding the identity of this Book, it is said to be the Preserved Tablet (اللوح المحفوظ) in which Allah wrote what was, what is, and what will be, which the angels of heaven read. It is also said to be the records of deeds (صحائف الأعمال) read by the angelic keepers of deeds. Others say it is the Quran, which Allah wrote in the Preserved Tablet. It is also said to be the Torah, which used to be written on parchment and unrolled for reading.

In view of the preceding verse, the last opinion is the most appropriate.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَالْبَيْتِ الْمَعْمُورِ** **by the Edifice inhabited,** It is said that the intended meaning is the Noble Kaaba, for it is the first house established for mankind and has remained inhabited (معمورا) since its establishment until this day of ours. The Exalted said: **إِنَّ أَوَّلَ بَيْتٍ وُضِعَ لِلنَّاسِ لَلَّذِي بِبَكَّةَ مُبارَكاً وَهُدىً لِلْعالَمِينَ** **Indeed the first house to be set up for mankind is the one at Bakkah, blessed and a guidance for all nations.** (3:96).

In the transmitted narrations, the Inhabited House (البيت المعمور) is a house in heaven directly above the Kaaba, visited by the angels.

The use of the indefinite form for **Book (كِتابٍ)** is to intimate its self-sufficiency from being defined; thus, it is an indefiniteness that implies and necessitates definiteness.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَالسَّقْفِ الْمَرْفُوعِ** **by the elevated roof,** This refers to the heaven.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَالْبَحْرِ الْمَسْجُورِ** **by the surging sea,** Al-Raghib said: **al-Sajr (السجر)** is the stirring up of fire. In *al-Majma‘*, it is said: **al-Masjur (المسجور)** means the filled; it is said: "I filled (سجرت) the oven," meaning I filled it with fire. The verse has been interpreted with both meanings. The first meaning is supported by His saying: **وَإِذَا الْبِحارُ سُجِّرَتْ** **when the seas are set afire,** (81:6), meaning kindled. It has been mentioned in the hadith that the seas will be kindled into fire on the Day of Resurrection. It is also said that the meaning is that their waters will recede by the kindling of fire within them.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **إِنَّ عَذابَ رَبِّكَ لَواقِعٌ ما لَهُ مِنْ دافِعٍ** **Indeed your Lord’s punishment will surely come to pass; there is none to avert it.** This is the response to the preceding oath. The punishment whose occurrence is announced is the punishment of the Day of Resurrection which Allah promised to the disbelieving deniers, as indicated by the following verse. In His saying: **ما لَهُ مِنْ دافِعٍ** **there is none to avert it,** there is an indication that it is part of the decreed judgment (القضاء المحتوم) whose occurrence is inevitable. The Exalted said: **وَأَنَّ السَّاعَةَ آتِيَةٌ لا رَيْبَ فِيها وَأَنَّ اللهَ يَبْعَثُ مَنْ فِي الْقُبُورِ** **and that the Hour is bound to come, there is no doubt about it, and that Allah will resurrect those who are in the graves.** (22:7).

In His saying: **عَذابَ رَبِّكَ** **your Lord’s punishment,** by attributing the punishment to "the Lord" (الرب) suffixed with the second-person pronoun rather than saying "the punishment of Allah," there is a support for the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله against those who deny his call, and a consolation for his soul that his Lord will not disgrace him on that day, as He said: **يَوْمَ لا يُخْزِي اللهُ النَّبِيَّ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ** **the day Allah will not let down the Prophet and the faithful who are with him.** (66:8).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **يَوْمَ تَمُورُ السَّماءُ مَوْراً وَتَسِيرُ الْجِبالُ سَيْراً** **The day the sky will be in a state of commotion, and the mountains will be in motion.** This is an adverbial phrase (ظرف) for His saying: **إِنَّ عَذابَ رَبِّكَ لَواقِعٌ** **Indeed your Lord’s punishment will surely come to pass.**

**Commotion (المور)** — according to *al-Majma‘* — is the agitation of a thing by going back and forth, like the agitation of smoke before it vanishes. Close to this is the saying of al-Raghib: that it is a swift flowing.

In any case, it contains an allusion to the folding of the celestial world as the Exalted mentions in various places of His speech, such as His saying: **إِذَا السَّماءُ انْفَطَرَتْ وَإِذَا الْكواكِبُ انْتَثَرَتْ** **When the sky is rent apart, and when the planets are scattered,** (82:1-2), and His saying: **يَوْمَ نَطْوِي السَّماءَ كَطَيِّ السِّجِلِّ لِلْكُتُبِ** **The day We shall roll up the sky like the rolling of the scrolls for writings.** (21:104), and His saying: **وَالسَّماواتُ مَطْوِيَّاتٌ بِيَمِينِهِ** **and the heavens will be folded up in His right hand.** (39:67).

Similarly, His saying: **وَتَسِيرُ الْجِبالُ سَيْراً** **and the mountains will be in motion,** is an allusion to the earthquake of the Hour on the earth, which the Exalted mentions in various places of His speech, such as His saying: **إِذا رُجَّتِ الْأَرْضُ رَجًّا وَبُسَّتِ الْجِبالُ بَسًّا فَكانَتْ هَباءً مُنْبَثًّا** **when the earth is shaken with a violent shaking, and the mountains are crumbled into pieces, and become scattered dust.** (56:4-6), and His saying: **وَسُيِّرَتِ الْجِبالُ فَكانَتْ سَراباً** **and the mountains will be set in motion and become a mirage.** (78:20).

## (Narrative Discussion)

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi: regarding His saying, the Exalted: **وَالطُّورِ وَكِتابٍ مَسْطُورٍ** — he said: "The Mount (الطور) is a mountain in Mount Sinai."

In *al-Majma‘*: **وَالْبَيْتِ الْمَعْمُورِ** — "It is a house in the fourth heaven directly above the Kaaba; the angels inhabit it through the worship they perform therein." This is narrated from Ibn Abbas and Mujahid. It is also narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه‌السلام, who said: **ويدخله كل يوم سبعون ألف ملك ثم لا يعودون إليه أبدا** — "Seventy thousand angels enter it every day, and then they never return to it again."

**I say:** The fact that the Inhabited House (البيت المعمور) is a house in heaven around which the angels circumambulate is mentioned in several traditions from the paths of both groups (Sunni and Shia), though they differ regarding its location. In most of them, it is in the fourth heaven; in some, it is in the first heaven; and in others, the seventh.

In the same source: **وَالسَّقْفِ الْمَرْفُوعِ** — "It is the heaven," narrated from Ali عليه‌السلام.

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi: **وَالسَّقْفِ الْمَرْفُوعِ** — he said: "The heaven." **وَالْبَحْرِ الْمَسْجُورِ** — he said: "It will be set afire on the Day of Resurrection."

In *al-Majma‘*: **وَالْبَحْرِ الْمَسْجُورِ** — "Meaning the filled," narrated from Qatadah. It is also said: "It is the kindled and heated like an oven," narrated from Mujahid, al-Dahhak, al-Akhfash, and Ibn Zayd. Then it was said: "The seas will be heated on the Day of Resurrection and turned into fires—then they will burst into one another, and then burst into the Fire." A hadith has been narrated to this effect.
